stocks.recent_news: Q3 FY26 (Dec 2025) results showed Revenue of Rs 1,098.52 Cr with Net Profit of Rs 13.57 Cr, down 35.07% YoY but with 21% sequential revenue growth. EBITDA margin expanded 140 bps QoQ to 14.9% and EBITDA grew 29% YoY. Aluminum forging/casting facilities commissioned, Mexico machining facility starting soon. Company reduced Rs 350 Cr debt this quarter, targeting debt below Rs 2,000 Cr by FY26 end. Share price around Rs 544 (April 10), P/E 40.76, market cap Rs 9,884 Cr.
stocks.sector_context: Indian forging/auto components sector expected to attract Rs 25,000-30,000 Cr in FY26 for capacity expansion and EV part localization. CV segment showed steady activity with H1 FY26 domestic sales of 4.63 lakh units. Forging sector transitioning toward lighter, EV-related components. Engine parts hold 28% share of auto components market.
stocks.peer_comparison: RKFORGE underperformed peers like Sona BLW on profit growth in Q3 FY26 due to international weakness offset by domestic strength. Operational improvements (margin expansion, debt reduction) compare favorably but earnings are weaker than diversified peers.
red_flags: Net profit fell 35% YoY in Q3 FY26
High P/E of 40.76 vs muted earnings
catalysts: Continued debt reduction toward sub-Rs 2,000 Cr target

stocks.recent_news: On April 1, 2026, JSW Energy received observation letters with no adverse observations from BSE/NSE on its scheme of arrangement with GE Power India Ltd; must file with NCLT within six months (by Oct 1, 2026). On April 9, 2026, exercised call option to acquire remaining 26% of JSW Mahanadi Power. JSW Mahanadi facing Rs 1,447.34 Cr water charge demand contested in Chhattisgarh High Court. Share price Rs 573 as of April 27, market cap Rs 1,00,833 Cr, P/E 44.3.
stocks.sector_context: Power sector outperforming broader market with stocks up to 30% YTD in 2026 versus 7.1% Nifty 50 dip. Hot summer driving peak power demand to 275-285 GW; analysts expect 8-12% YoY power demand growth. Adani Power +24% in April alone. Sector benefits from rising electricity consumption.
stocks.peer_comparison: JSW Energy trading at premium P/E (44.3) vs. NTPC and Tata Power. Some analysts prefer Tata Power and NTPC over JSW Energy on valuation. Adani Power has outperformed (+24% April, +51% 1Y) but JSW Energy participates in sector tailwind.
red_flags: Rs 1,447.34 Cr water charge demand at Mahanadi (under litigation)
US 26% reciprocal tariff creating FII outflow pressure on Indian equities
catalysts: GE Power India demerger NCLT filing (deadline Oct 1, 2026)
Hot summer driving power demand uptick

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (announced April 25): Net profit Rs 16,971 Cr down 12.55% YoY (vs Rs 19,407 Cr in Q4 FY25); revenue from operations up 12.86% YoY to Rs 2,98,621 Cr. Full-year FY26 gross revenue Rs 11,75,919 Cr (+9.8% YoY), EBITDA +13.5%. Board declared dividend of Rs 6/share. Jio: 16% subscriber base growth, 40% 5G user growth. Retail: highest-ever quarterly revenue Rs 98,000 Cr, +14% adjusted growth. Stock fell ~1.2% on April 27 to intraday low of Rs 1,312 post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Conglomerate exposure to O2C, retail, telecom (Jio), and oil & gas. West Asia war impacting O2C and oil & gas segment EBITDA. Refining margins under pressure globally. Telecom and digital businesses showing strong secular growth.
stocks.peer_comparison: Digital services and retail growth on par with leading consumer plays; O2C softness contrasts with stronger refining peers in some markets. Retail segment now competing with Trent, DMart.
red_flags: Q4 FY26 net profit dropped 12.55% YoY
O2C and oil & gas EBITDA hit by West Asia conflict
Stock under pressure after results, traded near intraday low
Dividend of Rs 6 modest given size

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting scheduled April 30, 2026 to approve FY26 audited results and consider final dividend - key near-term catalyst. On April 13, 2026, expanded into kitchen appliances and air cooler range under RR Signature brand (mixer grinders, electric cooktops, hand blenders). Net sales growth 17.2%, latest quarterly net sales Rs 2,535.86 Cr, PBDIT Rs 204.28 Cr. Share price Rs 1,508 as of April 28, market cap Rs 17,062 Cr, P/E 37.6. 1Y return +46.92%, 1M return +6.81%. Rated 'Buy' by MarketsMOJO on April 10.
stocks.sector_context: Cables/wires sector growing at double digits, projected Rs 1.3 lakh crore by FY29. Q1 FY26 sector leaders: Polycab revenue +25.71% YoY (profit +49.25%), KEI revenue +25.44% (profit +30.67%) - meaning RRKABEL's 17.2% growth lagged peers. Major sector overhang: UltraTech foray into wires/cables segment caused all listed players (Polycab, KEI, Havells, RRKABEL) to drop up to 15% earlier.
stocks.peer_comparison: RRKABEL revenue growth (17.2%) trails Polycab (+25.71%) and KEI (+25.44%) in Q1 FY26. Trades at lower P/E (37.6) vs. larger peers. FMEG diversification (now adding kitchen appliances) similar to Havells strategy.
red_flags: Revenue growth trailing larger cable peers (Polycab, KEI)
UltraTech's entry into wires/cables segment is structural sector overhang
catalysts: FY26 results and final dividend announcement on April 30, 2026
Kitchen appliances/air cooler launch under RR Signature

stocks.recent_news: Q1 CY2026 results (announced April 27): Consolidated revenue Rs 6,570 Cr (+18.1% YoY), net profit Rs 872.4 Cr, EBITDA Rs 1,528.93 Cr (+21% YoY from Rs 1,263.96 Cr). Consolidated volume +16.3% YoY to 363 million cases (India +14.4%, International +21.9%). Final dividend of Rs 0.50/share recommended, record date May 1, 2026, payment May 5. Stock has surged ~30% in April 2026 from Rs 384.10 (March 30 close) to Rs 501.80 (April 27). Recent acquisitions: Twizza (acquired), Crickley Dairy (agreement). Emkay Global maintained 'Buy', raised target by 15% to Rs 620 from Rs 540.
stocks.sector_context: Indian beverages industry estimated at Rs 3.8-4.2 trillion by 2026. CRISIL projects FMCG sector to grow 6-8% in FY26. Beverages highly weather-sensitive - hot summer 2026 with heatwaves driving strong tailwind for packaged water, juices, electrolyte drinks. Energy/sports drinks fastest-growing segment. VBL is largest PepsiCo franchisee outside USA.
stocks.peer_comparison: VBL outpacing FMCG sector growth (18.1% revenue vs 6-8% sector). Volume growth 16.3% well above category averages. International expansion (Africa via Twizza) differentiates from domestic-focused FMCG peers like HUL, Britannia.
red_flags: Stock already up 30% in April - valuation stretched after rally
Heavy summer dependence creates seasonality risk if monsoon arrives early
catalysts: Dividend record date May 1, payment May 5
